forecast-period.text: A trough will linger inland over the west until a cold front sweeps across southern SA on Thursday. This front will push the inland trough into the far north, exiting the state early Friday. A cooler southwest to southerly flow will gradually moderate in the wake of the front as a strong high pressure system moves in from the west from Friday, establishing in the Bight on Sunday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm later this evening.
West to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots offshore in the evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Mostly sunny. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots turning southeasterly during the evening.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 metres.
Cloudy.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots tending west to southwesterly below 10 knots during the afternoon and evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm.
West to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots south of Streaky Bay in the late evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres south of Streaky Bay.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres south of Streaky Bay.
Cloudy.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly during the morning.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres south of Streaky Bay.
Cloudy.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning westerly in the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm offshore later tonight.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 15 to 20 knots increasing to 15 to 25 knots in the middle of the day. Winds reaching up to 30 knots offshore in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to 20 to 25 knots in the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Cloudy.
North to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots tending west to northwesterly in the evening. Winds reaching up to 20 knots in the west in the late evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm inshore.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm inshore in the morning.

2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Cloudy.
North to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending north to northwesterly 15 to 20 knots in the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning westerly 20 to 25 knots in the late morning and early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm inshore in the morning and afternoon.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 30 knots in the late afternoon.
2.5 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots.
1.5 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 4 metres, increasing to 4 to 6 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly during the afternoon and evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 15 to 20 knots tending west to northwesterly 15 to 25 knots during the morning and early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 30 knots in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.text: Northerly 10 to 15 knots.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 15 to 25 knots tending west to northwesterly during the morning. Winds reaching up to 30 knots during the day.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 30 knots in the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 3 metres, tending westerly 3 to 4 metres during the morning.

2 to 2.5 metres.

Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.

Around 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Northwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning westerly 15 to 20 knots before dawn. Winds reaching up to 25 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon and evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning westerly 20 to 25 knots during the morning and early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 30 knots in the late evening.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots turning southwesterly in the late afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.

1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres inshore.
